criteria.label: Compute I(X; Y) from a 2×2 joint PMF by hand using the sum formula.
criteria.example: Joint [[0.5, 0], [0, 0.5]] (perfectly correlated bits): each term is 0.5 * log2(0.5/0.25) = 0.5 * 1, so I(X; Y) = 1 bit; verify via H(X)+H(Y)-H(X,Y) = 1+1-1.

criteria.label: Verify I(X; Y) = 0 for independent variables using entropy decomposition.
criteria.example: Independent fair coin X and Y: compute H(X)=1, H(Y)=1, H(X,Y)=2 bits; conclude I(X; Y) = 1+1-2 = 0.

criteria.label: Rank features with mutual_info_classif and interpret scores in bits.
criteria.example: On `make_classification(n_informative=3)`, run `mutual_info_classif`, print top-3 feature indices and scores; explain that higher I(X_i; Y) means more label uncertainty removed on average.

criteria.label: Contrast MI with Pearson correlation on a non-linear synthetic dataset.
criteria.example: Sample X ~ Uniform(-1,1), set Y = X^2; report corr(X,Y) ≈ 0 and estimated MI > 0 from binned or k-NN estimator; explain why MI detects the dependence.

criteria.label: State the data-processing inequality and apply it to a Markov chain X -> Y -> Z.
criteria.example: Given a pipeline where representations T = f(X) and labels Y depend on X only through T, argue I(X; Y) >= I(T; Y): processing through T cannot create information about Y beyond what T carries.
